notes: Distinguished and humble Christian servant of God mentioned by Augustine and Cassiodorus, noted for renouncing worldly honors for Christ.

docs.title: Letter 50027
docs.author: Augustine of Hippo
docs.subject_summary: 1. O excellent man and excellent brother, there was a time when you were unknown to my mind; and I charge my mind to bear patiently your being still unknown to my eyes, but it almost — nay, altogether
docs.excerpt: … and as so eminent a Christian and noble a man, would you really think it no disappointment to me if I were not allowed to see your home? How then can I help but mourn that I have not yet seen your face -- the dwelling-place of the mind I have come to know as though it were my own? 2.
